chief_complaint: A 21-year-old male patient presents with recurrent episodes of lost consciousness.
history: The patient has a history of recurrent episodes of lost consciousness that exhibited both features of seizure and syncope. Review of systems revealed stereotyped erectile dysfunction, characterized by unilateral penile tumescence (hemidystumescence) occurring peri-ictally. Neurologic workup revealed left temporal slowing on EEG.
exam: Vital signs: normal. Physical examination: normal.
labs: EEG: left temporal slowing. No other investigations mentioned.
